headline: The day Marine Le Pen took back control of her fate, despite a conviction
description: After being sentenced to one year of house arrest, the French far right's leader found an opening back into the presidential race.

description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Danish leader reflects on Donald Trump's statements at the NATO summit in Ankara regarding a potential US takeover of the island, as well as on the strategic autonomy for Europe that she is calling for.

headline: French parliamentary inquiry slams government's handling of British border
description: The report by a Communist MP denounces France's security-first approach as ineffective, dangerous and lacking democratic oversight.

headline: Tour de France: Kooij wins 5th stage in chaotic sprint finish
description: Olav Kooij, a 24-year-old Dutch debutant Tour contender, beat German Max Kanter into second, with Belgian Tim Merlier coming in third place for the Tour de France's fifth stage, after several riders crashed and others were slowed.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Recent FIFA decisions ramp up pressure on referees
description: From the Balogun case to the appointment of an all-Argentinian refereeing team for the France-Morocco match on Thursday, FIFA itself has shaken the refereeing corps, undermining their authority and fueling suspicions of bias.

description: Marine Le Pen announced she would run for president hours after her embezzlement conviction was upheld.

headline: Somaliland's high-risk quest for recognition
description: The self-proclaimed republic aspires to accede to the international stage. It finds itself at the center of a game of alliances, caught between Israeli, American and Emirati ambitions and threats from Yemen and neighboring Somalia.

headline: Albert Camus archives, including manuscripts, forged documents and letters, join national collection
description: The archives, previously kept within the writer's family, has been acquired by France's national library for €9 million with support from Hermès and CIC.
